entries.goal: Execute the fourth low-risk product-foundation documentation task for safety wording and economy guardrail alignment.
entries.notes: Documentation/register-only guardrail pass. Existing Mobile, Mission, Economy, Privacy and Legal planning sources now clarify that MVP/Beta uses internal points/XP and preview/display states only; real WFT/token/NFT/blockchain/wallet/payment/trading/payout/presale logic remains deferred and review_required; mobile must not activate token/presale/trading/NFT marketplace/wallet/payment features; rewards, XP, points, mission completion, leaderboards, inventory grants and rare items are not client-authoritative; server/backend/audit/ledger authority remains planned/review-required before real activation; missing or expensive items must offer fair playable detours before purchase pressure; health/child/location/camera/face/motion/privacy/consent expansion requires data-minimization, consent, fallback and human review. Runtime code, app/components/lib/functions, firestore.rules, runtime legal pages, public assets, package manifests, Unity/PR #13, auto-merge, auto-repair and deployment remained untouched.

entries.goal: Execute the fifth low-risk WellFit product-foundation task: Health, child, location, camera, face, motion and consent data-protection review pass.
entries.notes: Documentation/register-only data-protection review pass. Clarified that health/watch, child/minor/family, exact location/GPS/radius/safe-zone, camera/AR image, pose/face/biometric, motion/DeviceMotion/raw sensor, and consent/permission data are protected and review_required; require data minimization, consent, fallback, retention/deletion planning, and human/legal/privacy review; raw images/videos/face/biometric/health/location/child details are default do_not_store; protected data must not directly authorize rewards, mission completion, anti-cheat, leaderboards, inventory, rare items, token/payment/payout, or medical decisions. Runtime code, legal pages, consent flows, tracking, data collection, functions, rules, package manifests, public assets, Unity/PR #13, auto-merge, auto-repair, deployment, and reward/mission authority remained untouched.

Existing mobile planning now covers Android Chrome, Samsung Internet, iPhone Safari, desktop responsive smoke, /mobile route checklist, QR/PWA install flow, camera accepted/denied behavior, MediaPipe pose/face loading and fallbacks, DeviceMotion weak-context and permission limitations, WebGL/3D Flammi loading/fallback checks, visual smoke without strict pixel matching, and privacy/safety boundaries. entries.goal: Create a documentation/register-only manual evidence template for real Mobile/PWA device tests.
entries.notes: Documentation/register-only Mobile/PWA manual device evidence template added to the existing visual/device test plan. The template records test date, tester, device model, OS version, browser/app mode, route, expected and actual results, status, evidence reference, notes, privacy/safety notes, follow-up action, and severity. It covers Android Chrome, Samsung Internet, iPhone Safari, desktop responsive browser, existing /mobile routes, camera accepted/denied, pose/face/MediaPipe load/fail, DeviceMotion accepted/denied, WebGL/3D Flammi load/fallback, and PWA install/QR flow. Untested items remain device_test_required. Runtime/mobile/service-worker/public/product code, tracking/data collection, reward/mission authority, protected compliance areas, Unity/PR #13, auto-merge, auto-repair, and deployment remained untouched.

entries.goal: Create a concise human-friendly Mobile/PWA manual tester checklist handoff from the existing device evidence template.
entries.notes: Documentation/register-only Mobile/PWA tester checklist handoff added to the existing visual/device evidence plan. The handoff covers how to start testing with a placeholder preview URL, Android Chrome, Samsung Internet, iPhone Safari, desktop responsive browser, existing /mobile routes, permission surfaces, safe screenshot/recording boundaries, copy/paste result format, status/severity/follow-up rules, and keeps all real outcomes pending/device_test_required until a human tester reports them.
